methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
Climeworks, a Series F climate and energy technology company that has raised over $1.0B to date, is evaluated against a peer set of 4 same-sector comparables at similar or adjacent funding stages, supplemented by a broader universe of 8 private peers and 1 public reference point (Lnza). The immediate peer cohort spans TAE Technologies ($6.0B, Series G), Helion ($5.4B, Series F), Form Energy ($3.5B, Series G), and Watershed ($1.8B, Series G).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $1.0B total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F advancement, yielding $1.3B. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $4.5B, with a range spanning $1.8B to $6.0B. The dispersion reflects variation in technology maturity, revenue traction, and market positioning within the climate tech segment.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $5.1B (high), with a median of $2.9B. The significant gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects uncertainty in comparable company selection and the degree to which peer valuations reflect speculative capital deployment in frontier energy markets. Sensitivity to peer composition and stage-specific funding multiples remains material.
